creator: Carpetsmoker@rwxrwxrwx.net
text:
This patch resolves all the issues given by Erik.
I E-mail this to Erik on March 22nd, no reply so far.

>Fix:
diff -urN --exclude CVS /ports/misc/sonytv/Makefile ./Makefile
--- /ports/misc/sonytv/Makefile	Tue Dec 30 16:05:05 2003
+++ ./Makefile	Sun Apr  8 21:02:07 2007
@@ -7,25 +7,25 @@
 
 PORTNAME=	sonytv
 PORTVERSION=	1.0
+PORTREVISION=	1
 CATEGORIES=	misc tk83
 MASTER_SITES=	http://tools.ana.com/demos/
 
 MAINTAINER=	ports@FreeBSD.org
 COMMENT=	Sony-compatible TV remote
 
-RUN_DEPENDS=	wish8.3:${PORTSDIR}/x11-toolkits/tk83
-
-USE_X_PREFIX=	yes
-
 NO_BUILD=	yes
+USE_TK?=	83
 
-IS_INTERACTIVE=	yes
-NO_PACKAGE=	Hardcoded IR device name
-
-SCRIPTS_ENV=	CP="${CP}" RM="${RM}" SED="${SED}"
-
+PLIST_FILES=	bin/sonytv bin/sonytv+
 MAN1=		sonytv.1
 MLINKS=		sonytv.1 sonytv+.1
+
+post-patch:
+	@${CAT} ${PKGMESSAGE}
+	@${REINPLACE_CMD} -e 's|/usr/X11R6|${X11BASE}|; \
+		s|8.3|${TK_VER}|;' \
+			${WRKSRC}/sonytv ${WRKSRC}/sonytv+
 
 do-install:
 	${INSTALL_SCRIPT} ${WRKSRC}/sonytv ${PREFIX}/bin
diff -urN --exclude CVS /ports/misc/sonytv/files/patch-sony-tv ./files/patch-sony-tv
--- /ports/misc/sonytv/files/patch-sony-tv	Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
+++ ./files/patch-sony-tv	Sun Apr  8 21:02:07 2007
@@ -0,0 +1,162 @@
+--- sonytv.orig	Sun Jun 22 21:00:00 2003
++++ sonytv	Wed Mar 28 11:16:34 2007
+@@ -30,9 +30,6 @@
+ 
+ package require Tk 8.3
+ 
+-# edit as appropriate
+-set device {/dev/cuaa2}
+-
+ # work around bug in /usr/local/lib/tk8.3/tk.tcl
+ # if {[info exists tcl_platform(os)]} {
+ #   if {$tcl_platform(os) == "FreeBSD"} {
+@@ -180,14 +181,59 @@
+   flush $sir
+ }
+ 
+-if {[catch {set sir [open $device {WRONLY NOCTTY}]} why]} {
+-  # POSIX ENOENT => wrong platform or os? (e.g. Linux /dev/ttyS2)
+-  # POSIX EACCES => chmod or fbtab
+-  # POSIX ENXIO => check kernel configuration
+-  tk_messageBox -icon error -message "$why" \
+-    -title "Couldn't open IR port" -type ok
+-  exit 1
++proc optionwin {} {
++	global device color
++	toplevel .optwin
++	wm geometry .optwin 450x100
++	wm title .optwin Options
++
++	label .optwin.devl -text Device
++	entry .optwin.dev -textvariable device
++	label .optwin.colorl -text {Color scheme}
++	radiobutton .optwin.color1 -text Default -variable color -value 1
++	radiobutton .optwin.color2 -text {TK Default} -variable color -value 2
++	button .optwin.ok -text "Save" -command {saveoptions}
++	button .optwin.cancel -text "Close" -command {destroy .optwin}
++	
++	grid columnconfigure .optwin 2
++	grid .optwin.devl .optwin.dev -columnspan 2 -sticky w
++	grid .optwin.colorl .optwin.color1 .optwin.color2 -sticky w -columnspan 2
++	grid .optwin.ok .optwin.cancel -columnspan 2
++}
++
++proc testdev {device} {
++	if {[catch {set sir [open $device {WRONLY NOCTTY}]} why]} {
++		# POSIX ENOENT => wrong platform or os? (e.g. Linux /dev/ttyS2)
++	  # POSIX EACCES => chmod or fbtab
++		# POSIX ENXIO => check kernel configuration
++	  tk_messageBox -icon error -message "$why\nPlease edit ~/.sonytv" \
++		  -title "Couldn't open IR port" -type ok
++	}
++	return $sir
++}
++
++proc saveoptions {} {
++	global device color env
++
++	testdev $device
++	set file [open $env(HOME)/.sonytv w]
++	puts $file "# Set this to the device your remote is connected to.\nset device {$device}\n"
++	puts $file "# Set it to 1 for default colors, or 2 to the default TK colors.\nset color $color\n"
++	close $file
+ }
++
++
++if ![file exists $env(HOME)/.sonytv] {
++	exec touch $env(HOME)/.sonytv
++	set device {/dev/cuad0}
++	set color 1
++	saveoptions
++} else {
++	source $env(HOME)/.sonytv
++}
++
++set sir [testdev $device]
++
+ fconfigure $sir -buffering full -translation binary
+ if {$tcl_platform(platform) == "unix"} {
+   switch $tcl_platform(os) {
+@@ -205,19 +251,26 @@
+   fconfigure $sir -mode 115200,n,7,1
+ }
+ 
+-set gb {
+-  -background "#33cc33" \
+-  -activebackground "#66ff66" \
+-  -highlightcolor "#ff3333" \
+-  -highlightbackground black
++if {$color == 1} {
++	set gb {
++	  -background "#33cc33" \
++	  -activebackground "#66ff66" \
++	  -highlightcolor "#ff3333" \
++	  -highlightbackground black
++	}
++	set rb [concat {-foreground gray80 -activeforeground white} $gb]
++	set sb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -8 bold}}]
++	set cb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -18 bold italic}}]
++	set ub [concat {-activeforeground gray20} $gb \
++	  {-font {Courier -18 bold}}]
++} elseif {$color == 2} {
++	set gb {}
++	set rb {}
++	set sb { -font {Helvetica -8 bold} }
++	set cb { -font {Helvetica -18 bold italic} }
++	set ub { -font {Courier -18 bold} }
+ }
+ 
+-set rb [concat {-foreground gray80 -activeforeground white} $gb]
+-set sb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -8 bold}}]
+-set cb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -18 bold italic}}]
+-set ub [concat {-activeforeground gray20} $gb \
+-  {-font {Courier -18 bold}}]
+-
+ . configure -background gray20
+ eval button .muting -text MUTING -underline 0 $sb -command \{beam 20\}
+ eval button .ant -text ANT -underline 0 $sb -command \{beam 42\}
+@@ -267,6 +320,7 @@
+    0x14, 0x04, 0x1c, 0x04, 0x00, 0x00 };}
+ label .badge -image IR -borderwidth 0 -foreground black -background gray80
+ frame .sep3 -height 2 -relief ridge -borderwidth 1
++eval button .options -text Options $sb -command {optionwin}
+ label .logo -text PHONY -font { Times -14 bold } -state disabled \
+   -disabledforeground gray80 -background [. cget -background]
+ 
+@@ -286,8 +340,8 @@
+ grid .reset .down .menu
+ grid .sep2 -columnspan 3 -sticky ew -pady 5
+ grid .vol x .ch
+-grid .vol+ x .ch+
+-grid .vol- .badge .ch-
++grid .vol+ .badge .ch+
++grid .vol- .options .ch-
+ grid .sep3 -columnspan 3 -sticky ew -pady 5
+ grid .logo -columnspan 3
+ 
+@@ -346,7 +400,11 @@
+   <Prior> .ch+
+   <Next> .ch-
+ } {
+-  bind . $k [list tkButtonInvoke $b]
++	if {[info tclversion] == "8.4"} {
++		bind . $k [list tk::ButtonInvoke $b]
++	} else {
++		bind . $k [list tkButtonInvoke $b]
++	}
+ }
+ 
+ bind . Q {close $sir;destroy .}
+@@ -359,7 +417,11 @@
+ 
+ if {$tcl_platform(platform) == "unix"} {
+   # until I think up something clever
+-  wm iconbitmap . @/usr/X11R6/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32
++	if [file exists {/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32}] {
++		wm iconbitmap . @/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32
++	} elseif [file exists {/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32}] {
++		wm iconbitmap . @/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32
++	}
+ }
+ 
+ # Local Variables:
diff -urN --exclude CVS /ports/misc/sonytv/files/patch-sony-tv+ ./files/patch-sony-tv+
--- /ports/misc/sonytv/files/patch-sony-tv+	Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
+++ ./files/patch-sony-tv+	Sun Apr  8 21:02:07 2007
@@ -0,0 +1,162 @@
+--- sonytv+.orig	Sun Jun 22 21:00:00 2003
++++ sonytv+	Wed Mar 28 11:16:34 2007
+@@ -30,9 +30,6 @@
+ 
+ package require Tk 8.3
+ 
+-# edit as appropriate
+-set device {/dev/cuaa2}
+-
+ # work around bug in /usr/local/lib/tk8.3/tk.tcl
+ # if {[info exists tcl_platform(os)]} {
+ #   if {$tcl_platform(os) == "FreeBSD"} {
+@@ -180,14 +177,59 @@
+   flush $sir
+ }
+ 
+-if {[catch {set sir [open $device {WRONLY NOCTTY}]} why]} {
+-  # POSIX ENOENT => wrong platform or os? (e.g. Linux /dev/ttyS2)
+-  # POSIX EACCES => chmod or fbtab
+-  # POSIX ENXIO => check kernel configuration
+-  tk_messageBox -icon error -message "$why" \
+-    -title "Couldn't open IR port" -type ok
+-  exit 1
++proc optionwin {} {
++	global device color
++	toplevel .optwin
++	wm geometry .optwin 450x100
++	wm title .optwin Options
++
++	label .optwin.devl -text Device
++	entry .optwin.dev -textvariable device
++	label .optwin.colorl -text {Color scheme}
++	radiobutton .optwin.color1 -text Default -variable color -value 1
++	radiobutton .optwin.color2 -text {TK Default} -variable color -value 2
++	button .optwin.ok -text "Save" -command {saveoptions}
++	button .optwin.cancel -text "Close" -command {destroy .optwin}
++	
++	grid columnconfigure .optwin 2
++	grid .optwin.devl .optwin.dev -columnspan 2 -sticky w
++	grid .optwin.colorl .optwin.color1 .optwin.color2 -sticky w -columnspan 2
++	grid .optwin.ok .optwin.cancel -columnspan 2
++}
++
++proc testdev {device} {
++	if {[catch {set sir [open $device {WRONLY NOCTTY}]} why]} {
++		# POSIX ENOENT => wrong platform or os? (e.g. Linux /dev/ttyS2)
++	  # POSIX EACCES => chmod or fbtab
++		# POSIX ENXIO => check kernel configuration
++	  tk_messageBox -icon error -message "$why\nPlease edit ~/.sonytv" \
++		  -title "Couldn't open IR port" -type ok
++	}
++	return $sir
+ }
++
++proc saveoptions {} {
++	global device color env
++
++	testdev $device
++	set file [open $env(HOME)/.sonytv w]
++	puts $file "# Set this to the device your remote is connected to.\nset device {$device}\n"
++	puts $file "# Set it to 1 for default colors, or 2 to the default TK colors.\nset color $color\n"
++	close $file
++}
++
++
++if ![file exists $env(HOME)/.sonytv] {
++	exec touch $env(HOME)/.sonytv
++	set device {/dev/cuad0}
++	set color 1
++	saveoptions
++} else {
++	source $env(HOME)/.sonytv
++}
++
++set sir [testdev $device]
++
+ fconfigure $sir -buffering full -translation binary
+ if {$tcl_platform(platform) == "unix"} {
+   switch $tcl_platform(os) {
+@@ -205,19 +247,26 @@
+   fconfigure $sir -mode 115200,n,7,1
+ }
+ 
+-set gb {
+-  -background "#33cc33" \
+-  -activebackground "#66ff66" \
+-  -highlightcolor "#ff3333" \
+-  -highlightbackground black
++if {$color == 1} {
++	set gb {
++	  -background "#33cc33" \
++	  -activebackground "#66ff66" \
++	  -highlightcolor "#ff3333" \
++	  -highlightbackground black
++	}
++	set rb [concat {-foreground gray80 -activeforeground white} $gb]
++	set sb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -8 bold}}]
++	set cb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -18 bold italic}}]
++	set ub [concat {-activeforeground gray20} $gb \
++	  {-font {Courier -18 bold}}]
++} elseif {$color == 2} {
++	set gb {}
++	set rb {}
++	set sb { -font {Helvetica -8 bold} }
++	set cb { -font {Helvetica -18 bold italic} }
++	set ub { -font {Courier -18 bold} }
+ }
+ 
+-set rb [concat {-foreground gray80 -activeforeground white} $gb]
+-set sb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -8 bold}}]
+-set cb [concat $rb {-font {Helvetica -18 bold italic}}]
+-set ub [concat {-activeforeground gray20} $gb \
+-  {-font {Courier -18 bold}}]
+-
+ . configure -background gray20
+ eval button .muting -text MUTING -underline 0 $sb -command \{beam 20\}
+ eval button .ant -text ANT -underline 0 $sb -command \{beam 42\}
+@@ -279,6 +328,7 @@
+ eval button .pic- -text PICTURE- -underline 7 $sb -command \{beam 25\}
+ eval button .pic+ -text PICTURE+ -underline 7 $sb -command \{beam 24\}
+ eval button .test -text TEST $sb -command \{beam 127\}
++eval button .options -text Options $sb -command {optionwin}
+ frame .sep4 -height 2 -relief ridge -borderwidth 1
+ label .logo -text PHONY -font { Times -14 bold } -state disabled \
+   -disabledforeground gray80 -background [. cget -background]
+@@ -299,8 +349,8 @@
+ grid .reset .down .menu
+ grid .sep2 -columnspan 3 -sticky ew -pady 5
+ grid .vol x .ch
+-grid .vol+ x .ch+
+-grid .vol- .badge .ch-
++grid .vol+ .badge .ch+
++grid .vol- .options .ch-
+ grid .sep3 -columnspan 3 -sticky ew -pady 5
+ grid .erase .add .autopgm
+ grid .time .cable .notch
+@@ -380,7 +430,11 @@
+   X .exit
+   x .exit
+ } {
+-  bind . $k [list tkButtonInvoke $b]
++	if {[info tclversion] == "8.4"} {
++		bind . $k [list tk::ButtonInvoke $b]
++	} else {
++		bind . $k [list tkButtonInvoke $b]
++	}
+ }
+ 
+ bind . Q {close $sir;destroy .}
+@@ -393,7 +447,11 @@
+ 
+ if {$tcl_platform(platform) == "unix"} {
+   # until I think up something clever
+-  wm iconbitmap . @/usr/X11R6/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32
++	if [file exists {/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32}] {
++		wm iconbitmap . @/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32
++	} elseif [file exists {/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32}] {
++		wm iconbitmap . @/usr/local/include/X11/bitmaps/xlogo32
++	}
+ }
+ 
+ # More SIRCS codes:
diff -urN --exclude CVS /ports/misc/sonytv/pkg-message ./pkg-message
--- /ports/misc/sonytv/pkg-message	Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
+++ ./pkg-message	Sun Apr  8 22:56:34 2007
@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+======================================================================
+FreeBSD Tcl 8.4 packages are built without serial channel support, If
+you want to use Tcl 8.4 with sonytv then you will have to install
+Tcl 8.4 from the ports collection.
+Tcl 8.3 packages are fine.
+
+How to tell if your Tcl 8.4 has serial channel support:
+
+[BROKEN]
+$ tclsh8.4
+% fconfigure [open /dev/cuad0 RDWR]
+-blocking 1 -buffering full -buffersize 4096 -encoding iso8859-1
+-eofchar {{} {}} -translation {auto lf}
+% exit
+ 
+[CORRECT]
+$ tclsh8.4
+% fconfigure [open /dev/cuad0 RDWR]
+-blocking 1 -buffering full -buffersize 4096 -encoding iso8859-1
+-eofchar {{} {}} -translation {auto crlf} -mode 9600,n,8,1 -xchar { }
+% exit 
+
+(replace "cuad0" with any serial device file you have read/write
+access to)
+======================================================================
diff -urN --exclude CVS /ports/misc/sonytv/pkg-plist ./pkg-plist
--- /ports/misc/sonytv/pkg-plist	Tue Dec 30 16:05:05 2003
+++ ./pkg-plist	Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
@@ -1,2 +0,0 @@
-bin/sonytv
-bin/sonytv+
diff -urN --exclude CVS /ports/misc/sonytv/scripts/configure ./scripts/configure
--- /ports/misc/sonytv/scripts/configure	Tue Dec 30 16:05:05 2003
+++ ./scripts/configure	Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
@@ -1,22 +0,0 @@
-#!/bin/sh
-cd ${WRKSRC:?Uh-oh.}||exit 1
-umask 022
-/usr/bin/dialog \
---title "Where's your IR device?" \
---radiolist "Select a serial port:" 12 48 4 \
-/dev/cuaa0 "" off \
-/dev/cuaa1 "e.g. Toshiba Libretto" off \
-/dev/cuaa2 "e.g. Sony VAIO F-series" on \
-/dev/cuaa3 "" off \
-2>.portstmp.$$
-case $? in
-0) read device <.portstmp.$$
-  for f in sonytv sonytv+
-  do
-    ${SED} -e "/set device {/s;{.*};{${device}};" $f >.portstmp.$$
-    /usr/bin/cmp -s .portstmp.$$ $f||${CP} .portstmp.$$ $f
-  done
-  ${RM} -f .portstmp.$$ ;;
-*) ${RM} -f .portstmp.$$
-  exit 1 ;;
-esac
